info.title: Polaris API
info.description:
# Polaris Technology Catalog API

The Polaris API provides programmatic access to the technology catalog, enabling automation, integration, and custom tooling.

## Key Features

- **RESTful Design** - Resource-based URLs with proper HTTP methods
- **Comprehensive Coverage** - Systems, Teams, Technologies, Components, Policies, and more
- **Developer-Friendly** - Clear error messages and consistent response structure

## Authentication

Most endpoints require authentication using session-based authentication integrated with the web application.

**Authorization Levels:**
- **Public** - No authentication required
- **Authenticated** - Valid user session required  
- **Team Member** - User must belong to a team
- **Team Owner** - User must belong to team that owns the resource
- **Superuser** - User must have superuser role

## Response Format

All successful responses follow this structure:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": [...],
  "count": 10
}
```

All errors follow this structure:
```json
{
  "statusCode": 404,
  "message": "Resource 'example' not found"
}
```

## Richardson Maturity Model

This API implements **RMM Level 2** with proper use of HTTP methods and status codes.

type.description: Reference type (vcs, website, documentation, issue-tracker, etc.)

scope.description: Dependency scope when unambiguous across known system usage

isDirect.description: Always true for direct dependencies returned by component detail

scope.description: Dependency scope from the system USES edge when a system filter is active

isDirect.description: True when this dependency is a direct child of the queried component

isCircular.description: True when this node repeats an ancestor in the dependency path

hasCircularDependencies.description: Whether any returned path contains a circular dependency

truncated.description: Whether the result was truncated by the node limit

packageManager.description: Package manager (npm, maven, pypi, cargo, etc.)

systemCount.description: Number of systems using this component

get.description: Returns the version of the running application, baked in at build time.

post.summary: Create a new technology from an unlinked component
post.description:
Creates a new technology by claiming an existing, currently-unlinked Component —
a Technology can never exist without at least one linked Component. All Component
nodes sharing `componentName` that aren't already linked to a Technology are
linked in the same operation. For technology that can never be observed by SBOM
scanning (databases, cloud services, etc.), use POST /platforms instead.

Superuser only — this mirrors the gate already on linking a component to an
*existing* technology (POST /technologies/{name}/components), so creating a new
one isn't a lower bar than linking to one. The intended entry point is the
guided component-links queue (/admin/component-links), not this endpoint directly.

componentName.description: Name of an existing, unlinked Component — all currently-unlinked versions sharing this name are linked

404.description: No unlinked component with the given name was found

get.description: Retrieves a list of all technologies with their versions and approvals

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring match on technology name

post.description: Creates a new team. Requires superuser access.

get.description: Retrieves a list of all teams with their technology and system counts

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring match on team name

post.description: Creates a new system with optional repositories

get.description: Retrieves a list of all systems with their metadata, component counts, and repository counts

post.description:
Validates a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) in CycloneDX or SPDX format.
Requires authentication via session or API token.

repositoryUrl.description: URL to the SCM repository that produced this SBOM

sbom.description: The SBOM document in CycloneDX or SPDX format

400.description: Bad request - invalid JSON or missing required fields

404.description: Not Found - system with repository URL not found

message.example: No system found with repository URL: https://github.com/org/repo. Please create the system first.

415.description: Unsupported Media Type - Content-Type must be application/json

get.description: Retrieves a list of all repositories with their metadata and system counts

post.description:
Creates a manually-declared platform entry — infrastructure or services (e.g. PostgreSQL, Kubernetes)
that can never be observed by SBOM scanning. Unlike Technology, a Platform carries no evidence
requirement, so creation is deliberately restricted to superusers.

get.description: Retrieves a list of manually-declared platforms (infrastructure/services not observable via SBOM scanning) with their approvals

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring match on platform name

get.description: Retrieves a list of all licenses discovered in components with usage statistics

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by license ID or name

componentCount.description: Number of components using this license

get.description: Checks the health of the API and database connectivity
Retrieves a list of components with their metadata and system usage counts. Supports filtering and pagination.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by component name, group, or package URL (case-insensitive)

parameters.name: packageManager
parameters.description: Filter by package manager (e.g., npm, maven, pypi, nuget, cargo)

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by component type (e.g., library, framework, application)

parameters.name: license
parameters.description: Filter by license SPDX ID (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

parameters.name: hasLicense
parameters.description: Filter by license presence (ignored when license is also specified)

parameters.name: system
parameters.description: Filter by system name (only components used by this system)

parameters.name: direct
parameters.description: When true, restrict to direct dependencies. With system, applies to that system; otherwise applies across all systems.

parameters.name: includeDev
parameters.description: When false, hide dependencies whose matching USES edge is dev-scoped.

parameters.name: depScope
parameters.description: Filter by dependency scope on the USES edge (requires system)

total.description: Total count of components matching filters (without pagination)

get.description:
Retrieves audit log entries with optional filtering.
Returns the most recent entries first.

parameters.name: entityType
parameters.description: Filter by entity type (e.g., VersionConstraint, Technology)

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: Filter by operation (e.g., ACTIVATE, DEACTIVATE, CREATE)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of entries to return

get.description:
Checks if a technology (and optionally a specific version) is approved for a team.

**Approval Hierarchy:**
1. Version-specific approval (highest priority) - `level: "version"`
2. Technology-level approval - `level: "technology"`
3. Default (not approved) - `level: "default"`, `time: "eliminate"`

**TIME Framework Values:**
- `adopt` - Recommended for use
- `trial` - Experimental use allowed
- `assess` - Under evaluation
- `hold` - Do not use for new projects
- `eliminate` - Must be removed

parameters.name: environment
parameters.description: System environment to check. Environment-specific approval takes precedence over a blanket approval for the same team.

400.description: Team and technology parameters are required

get.summary: Get version sprawl counts by severity
get.description: Returns counts of detected version sprawl groups by severity tier, for dashboard summary widgets.

Detects technologies with 2+ distinct versions in use as direct dependencies across systems, scored and ranked by sprawl severity (version count, affected system count, major-version spread, and EOL exposure). Transitive-only versions are excluded since a team cannot act on a version it doesn't declare itself.

parameters.name: severity
parameters.description: Filter to a single severity tier

get.description: Retrieves a list of all users with their team counts (requires superuser role)

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Case-insensitive search on user email or name

count.description: Number of items in current page

get.description: Returns full user details including teams, tokens (technical users), and recent audit activity. Superuser only.

get.summary: Get technologies shaped for the radar visualization
get.description: Returns all technologies with a resolved TIME value for the radar view. When `team` is provided each technology is placed in the ring matching that team's TIME approval. Without `team` the dominant TIME value across all approvals is used (majority vote; ties broken by severity). Technologies with no applicable approval are marked 'unclassified'.

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Filter TIME values by this team name

put.description: Updates a technology's properties and ownership. Requires superuser role or membership in the technology's owner team.

get.description:
Retrieves detailed information about a specific technology including versions, components, systems, policies, and approvals.

Returns comprehensive data about:
- Technology metadata (type, vendor, risk level)
- All versions with release and EOL dates
- Components using this technology
- Systems that depend on it
- Applicable policies
- Technology-level and version-specific approvals

delete.description: Deletes a technology and all its relationships. Requires superuser role or membership in the technology's owner team.

403.description: Forbidden - user is not a superuser and does not belong to the owner team

post.summary: Link a component to a technology
post.description: Creates an IS_VERSION_OF relationship between a component and a technology. Accepts either `purl` (PURL-based, preferred for SBOM-sourced components) or `componentName` + `componentVersion` (legacy, name+version match). When `purl` is provided the call requires superuser access and also refreshes Team→Technology USES edges for all affected systems.

post.summary: Set a team's TIME approval for a technology
post.description: Creates or updates a team's APPROVES relationship on a technology. The user must be a member of the specified team or a superuser.

403.description: User is not a member of the specified team

put.description: Updates a team's email and/or responsibility area. Requires superuser access.

422.description: At least one field to update is required

get.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific team including technology ownership, system ownership, and member count

delete.description:
Deletes a team and all its relationships. Requires superuser access.

**Authorization:** Superuser

**Business Rules:**
- Team cannot be deleted if it owns any systems
- All systems must be reassigned or deleted first

409.description: Cannot delete team that owns systems

example.message: Cannot delete team 'frontend-team' because it owns 3 system(s). Please reassign or delete the systems first.

get.description:
Retrieves all technologies used by a team with usage statistics and compliance status.

**Compliance Status:**
- `compliant`: Technology is approved (invest/tolerate)
- `unapproved`: No approval exists
- `migration-needed`: Technology is marked for migration
- `violation`: Technology is marked for elimination

get.summary: Get the compliance scorecard for a team
get.description:
Computes an on-demand governance scorecard from existing compliance data:
SBOM freshness across owned systems, Eliminate-marked technologies in use,
disallowed-license usage, critical version constraint violations, and TIME
classification coverage of used technologies.

Retrieves all technology approvals for a specific team.

Returns both technology-level and version-specific approvals with TIME framework values.

put.description:
Replaces all system fields. All required fields must be provided.

**Authorization:** Team Owner - User must belong to the team that owns the system

400.description: System name is required or missing required fields

403.description: User does not belong to team that owns this system

patch.description:
Updates specific fields of a system. Only provided fields are updated.

**Authorization:** Team Owner - User must belong to the team that owns the system

403.description: User does not belong to team that owns this system

get.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific system

delete.description: Deletes a system and all its relationships (requires authorization and team ownership)

403.description: Forbidden - user's team does not own this system

get.summary: Get the compliance scorecard for a system
get.description:
Computes an on-demand governance scorecard from existing compliance data:
SBOM freshness, Eliminate-marked technologies in use, disallowed-license
usage, critical version constraint violations, and TIME classification
coverage of used technologies.

post.summary: Register a repository for a system
post.description: Links a repository to a system for SBOM tracking. Creates the repository if it doesn't exist.

name.description: Repository name (auto-extracted from URL if not provided)

get.description: Returns all repositories linked to a system

get.summary: Get active issues for a system
get.description:
Returns components in the system that have known vulnerabilities,
disallowed licenses, or health problems (EOL, deprecated, stale maintenance).

get.summary: Get dependency graph data for a system
get.description:
Returns all nodes and edges for the system dependency graph.
Nodes include the system, packageManager group nodes, individual
component nodes, and technology nodes. Expand/collapse state is
managed client-side.

put.description: Updates a platform's properties and stewardship. Requires superuser role or membership in the platform's steward team.

get.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific platform, including its steward team and TIME approvals.

delete.description: Deletes a platform and all its relationships. Requires superuser role or membership in the platform's steward team.

403.description: Forbidden - user is not a superuser and does not belong to the steward team

post.summary: Set a team's TIME approval for a platform
post.description: Creates or updates a team's APPROVES relationship on a platform. The user must be a member of the specified team or a superuser.

403.description: User is not a member of the specified team

post.summary: Generate an API token for the authenticated user
post.description:
Creates a new API token for the currently authenticated user.
The plaintext token is returned only once in the response.
Maximum 10 active tokens per user.

400.description: Missing description or token cap reached

get.description: Returns all tokens belonging to the authenticated user (without hashes).

delete.description:
Revokes the specified token. Ownership is enforced — users can only
revoke their own tokens.

404.description: Token not found or not owned by the current user

get.description: Returns components using disallowed licenses. Supports pagination, sorting, and search.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by component name, license ID, system name, or team name (case-insensitive)

parameters.name: direct
parameters.description: When true, restrict to direct dependencies only

parameters.name: depScope
parameters.description: Filter by dependency scope on the USES edge

get.description: Retrieves organization-wide license statistics including counts by category, OSI approval, and deprecation status

Retrieves detailed information about a specific license including
usage statistics and full license text from the SPDX license list.
Returns paginated list of components that use the specified license
Returns dashboard-ready health, advisory, freshness, and critical-system risk aggregates.
Returns components and linked technologies already unsupported or past EOL.
Returns components and linked technologies approaching EOL within the configured warning window.
Returns unlinked, non-dismissed components with fuzzy Technology name suggestions. Superuser only.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring match on component name

get.description: Retrieves components grouped by package manager, group, and name. Each group includes ordered version details for version-specific actions.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by component name, group, or package URL (case-insensitive)

parameters.name: hasLicense
parameters.description: Filter by license presence (ignored when license is also specified)

parameters.name: system
parameters.description: Filter to groups where any version is used by this system

parameters.name: direct
parameters.description: When true, restrict matching to direct dependencies. With system, applies to that system; otherwise applies across all systems.

parameters.name: includeDev
parameters.description: When false, hide dependencies whose matching USES edge is dev-scoped.

parameters.name: depScope
parameters.description: Filter matching by dependency scope on the USES edge (requires system)

parameters.name: lifecycleRisk
parameters.description: When true, restrict to components returned by approaching or expired EOL rollups.

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: Field to sort grouped components by

total.description: Total count of unique component groups matching filters

get.description: Retrieves read-only lifecycle visibility from endoflife.date for a component version.

post.summary: Dismiss a component from the link suggestions queue
post.description: Marks a component (all versions) as intentionally not linked to a Technology. Superuser only.

componentName.description: The component name to dismiss (all versions)

get.description:
Returns the description for a package from its public registry (npm, PyPI,
NuGet, Cargo, etc.). Results are cached server-side for 1 hour. Returns null
when the registry is unsupported or the package has no description.

parameters.name: packageManager
parameters.description: Package manager (npm, pypi, maven, nuget, cargo, golang)

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Package group or scope (e.g. npm scope without @, Maven groupId)

get.description: Retrieves a single component by its encoded component identity key.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Encoded component identity from the components list.

400.description: Component key is missing or invalid

get.description: Retrieves direct and transitive dependencies for a component. Scope filtering requires a system context because dependency scope is stored on System USES Component relationships.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Encoded component identity from the components list.

parameters.name: system
parameters.description: Restrict dependencies to components used by this system.

400.description: Component key or query parameters are invalid

get.description:
Retrieves all compliance violations across the organization.

A compliance violation occurs when:
- A team uses a technology without approval (unapproved)
- A team uses a technology marked for elimination (eliminated)

Results include affected systems and migration targets where applicable.

NextAuth.js authentication endpoints for OAuth providers.

**Available Providers:**
- GitHub OAuth

**Endpoints:**
- `GET /api/auth/signin` - Sign in page
- `GET /api/auth/signout` - Sign out
- `GET /api/auth/callback/{provider}` - OAuth callback
- `GET /api/auth/session` - Get current session
- `GET /api/auth/csrf` - Get CSRF token
- `GET /api/auth/providers` - List available providers

**Authorization Levels:**
- `user` - Default role for authenticated users
- `superuser` - Admin role (configured via SUPERUSER_EMAILS env var)

302.description: Redirect to OAuth provider or callback

post.description:
Creates a pending user record for a GitHub username and returns a
shareable invite link. The user is activated when they first sign in
via GitHub OAuth and their username matches the pending record.

**Authorization:** Superuser

409.description: A pending invite already exists for this username

post.description: Creates a non-OAuth technical user for API access. Superuser only.

get.description:
Retrieves all users with their team memberships and management permissions.

**Authorization:** Superuser

post.summary: Generate an API token for a technical user
post.description:
Creates a new API token for the specified technical user.
The plaintext token is returned only once in the response.
Superuser only.

get.summary: List API tokens for a user
get.description: Returns all tokens for the specified user (without hashes). Superuser only.

post.description:
Assigns a user to one or more teams, replacing existing team memberships.

**Authorization:** Superuser

**Behavior:**
- Removes all existing team memberships
- Creates new memberships for specified teams
- Optionally grants team management permissions

canManage.description: Array of team names user can manage (optional)

400.description: User ID or teams array is required

put.description:
Grants or revokes superuser access for a user.

**Authorization:** Superuser

**Constraints:**
- A superuser cannot demote themselves

400.description: Invalid role value or missing parameters

403.description: Superuser access required, or attempting to demote yourself

delete.description: Deletes a technical user and all associated tokens. Superuser only.
Revokes the specified API token. Superuser only.

put.description:
Updates allowed status for one or multiple licenses.
Supports both single license and bulk operations.

**Authorization:** Superuser only

400.description: Invalid request body or missing license data

get.description:
Retrieves all licenses with allowed status for superadmin management.
Includes filtering and search capabilities.

**Authorization:** Superuser only

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by license ID or name

post.summary: Import a system from a GitHub repository
post.description:
Fetches repository metadata and dependency manifests from the GitHub API,
generates an SBOM via cdxgen, creates the system with its repository,
and ingests the SBOM. Superuser only.

repositoryUrl.description: GitHub repo URL or owner/repo shorthand

systemName.description: Optional system name override (defaults to repository name)

422.description: GitHub repository not accessible or no manifests found

post.summary: Start a GitHub owner import job
post.description:
Lists repositories for a GitHub organization or user and starts a background import job.
Returns immediately with a job ID that can be polled for progress.

owner.description: GitHub organization/user login or profile URL

repositories.description: Selected repositories to import. When omitted, all matching owner repositories are imported.

post.description:
Lists repositories for a GitHub organization or user before starting an import job.
Superusers can use the returned list to choose which repositories to import.

owner.description: GitHub organization/user login or profile URL
